slides.text: The ficitonal land of Arithmetica contains various sections with different stories. Each section focuses on a different mathematical concept, and each story's plot is lead by a young explorer from Arithmetica, Luna.

Students will follow Luna on her journey and experience every adventure by her side. They will not only enter a new fictional world, but also learn and practice the fundamental concepts of primary-level mathematics.

This is a new approach to learning that combines both entertainment (through the narrative), but also teaching. It is designed to help children look at maths another way, not only through the angle of text books and tests.

Students are prompted to look at maths as a useful tool for solving problems in real life, that do not necessarily have to be complex, and think about new concepts in ways that the standard school book doesn't explain.

This is all done in the hope that students will not only understand new concepts, but will understand the usefulness of math, and slowly develop a passion for it. Even for the ones not so fond of the subject, the combination of stories with the educational material provides a more pleasing way to work with it.

It should be noted that these stories do not cover in any way the whole syllabus for primary school maths, nor are they currently enough on their own for a child to establish the complete mathematical knowledge of the primary level. They are to be used as enhancing teaching tools, to perhaps introduce a topic, or for additional practice.

The next steps of the site include to unlock more magical sections with their respective stories and math topics, to include more questions for better practice and solutions worked out step-by-step. Please consider that this is a new model, created by high school students, and hence is limited to specific resources. Therefore, its growth will take place gradually over time.
